characters.name: Ame-No-Uzume
characters.description: Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto(Japanese:天宇受売命,天鈿女命) is thegoddessofdawn, mirth, meditation, revelry and the arts in theShintoreligion ofJapan, and the wife of fellow-godSarutahiko Ōkami. (-no-Mikoto is a common honorific appended to the names of Japanese gods; it may be understood as similar to the English honorific 'the Great'.) She famously helped draw out the missing sun deity,Amaterasu Omikami, when she had hidden herself in a cave. Her name can also be pronounced asAma-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. She is also known as Ōmiyanome-no-Ōkami, aninarikamipossibly due to her relationship with her husband. She is also known as Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, The Great Persuader, and The Heavenly Alarming Female.She is depicted inkyōgenfarce as Okame, a woman who revels in her sensuality.[citation needed

characters.name: American entities
characters.description: – Whiskey Jack's description of the Native American religion Outside of theOld Godsand theNew Gods, North America is home to a wide array ofsupernaturalbeings native to its land. These entities were born out of the belief and legends of the Indigenous American population, however they do not identify as gods. As Whiskey Jack says, America is a "bad land" for the gods, and thus the Native Americans did not create deities but rather "culture heroes" and "creator spirits". They do not need worship to survive, and are tied to the American land the same way theOriginal godsare tied to their homelands: Whiskey Jack explains that the Native Americans treated the land as their church and thus the native American entities will be here as long as the American land is here.

characters.name: Bill Sanders
characters.description: Bill SandersExternal linksBill Sanders, full nameWilliam Huntington Sanders, was a repairman who was contacted byBilquisin the episodeSerious Moonlight. Bill angered Bilquis by touching her shoulder. He told her that he was the owner of the biggest IT company in the world, and he thought that as a goddess, Bilquis would be an ideal showgirl for his business. As Bilquis angrily refuses, Bill tells that he knew what kind of person she was. Bilquis brings him to her bedroom to show her power by having sex with him. Bilquis rides on top of Bill, taunting him as he slowly slides into her body. Bill disappears into her vagina, killing him. However, her body seemed to reject absorbing Bill, causing her to vomit.

characters.description: Bilquis (Novel)BPALGraphic novelBiographical informationAliasesQueen of ShebaGenderFemalePantheonArabianTypeJinnOccupationProstituteChronological informationFirst AppearanceAmerican Gods,Chapter OneExternal links  –Bilquis,Interlude 1

characters.name: Bilquis/Series
characters.description: Bilquis (Series)Biographical informationAliasesQueen of ShebaGenderFemalePantheonArabianTypeOld godPersonal informationAffiliationNew GodsChronological informationFirst Appearance"The Bone Orchard"Last Appearance"Sister Rising"PortrayalPortrayed byYetide BadakiExternal linksIMDbTwitterWikipedia  –Mr. Nancyabout Bilquis,Come to Jesus ForSeason One, Yetide Badaki describes Bilquis as an ancient goddess of love trying to find her relevance in the present day with online dating, an "extraordinary character in an ordinary situation" and trying to go through life day by day. In a time where millennials have less and less sex, and less and less intimacy, she is starving and basically feeding off "empty calories", since most of what is sold as "love" today is actually "empty containers", leaving you feeling empty no matter how much you buy.[1]

characters.name: Chad Mulligan
characters.description: Chad MulliganBiographical informationGenderMaleTypeHumanOccupationChief of Lakeside PoliceChronological informationFirst AppearanceSeason ThreePortrayalPortrayed byEric JohnsonExternal linksIMDbTwitterWikipedia Chad Mulliganis the Chief of Police inLakeside, Wisconsin whereShadowis temporarily staying. Mulligan isLakeside’s Chief of Police who “spends his days mediating disputes between townsfolk he’s known his whole life. This trademark low-key diplomacy works well for Chad until a local teenage girl goes missing, igniting a panic of paranoia and blame that threatens to tear Chad’s beloved town apart.”[1]

characters.name: Chad Mulligan

characters.name: Chad Mulligan/Novel
characters.description: Chad MulliganBPALGraphic novelBiographical informationGenderMaleTypeHumanOccupationChief of Lakeside PolicePersonal informationFamilyAudrey Burton(distant cousin)Chronological informationFirst AppearanceChapter TenExternal linksChad Mulliganis the Chief of Police inLakeside, Wisconsin whereShadowis temporarily staying. He first appears inChapter Tenwhen he offers a freezing Shadow a ride into town and introduces him to the townsfolk. TBA Shadowattempts to walk intoLakesidethe first morning he arrives but underestimates how cold it is. Just as he approaches the bridge into Lakeside, Chief of Police Chad Mulligan pulls up next to him and offers him a ride into town. He takes Shadow to Mabel's diner for breakfast and Mulligan offers to help Shadow find a car. He then takes Shadow shopping for clothes and groceries as they talk about Mike Ainsel's "uncle"Emerson. Mulligan then drops Shadow off to buy a purple jeep from Missy Gunther.

characters.name: Cordelia
characters.description: CordeliaBiographical informationGenderFemalePersonal informationAlliesMr. WednesdayChronological informationFirst AppearanceA Winter's TaleLast AppearanceAshes and DemonsPortrayalPortrayed byAshley ReyesExternal linksIMDb Cordeliaroad trips across the country meeting herboss’eccentric friends and helping him with “tech stuff,” unaware she’s caught up in a divine battle for the soul of America. Cordelia was a whip-smart but rebellious college dropout facing mounting legal bills and student debt beforeMr. Wednesdayarrived in her life.[1][2]Mr. Wednesday wrangles her to his side to help with tech stuff, though she remains unaware of his divinity.[3]

characters.name: Deities
characters.description: DeitiesBiographical informationAliasesGods, Goddesses, demi-gods, mythical creaturesBirthplaceEarthOccupationDeities, demigods, mythical creaturesPersonal informationAffiliationOld GodsNew GodsOther CharactersExternal links Deitiesare powerful anthropomorphic beings that have control over various aspects of the natural world as well as human endeavors. They are thought-forms created by the collective belief of humanity and by the worship and rituals of religions around the world; given life and divine purpose through the supernatural powers invoked by genuine mortal faith. The gods are the main focus of both theAmerican Godsnoveland itsTV seriesadaptation. "Old Gods" is a term used inAmerican Godsto refer to all the gods and affiliated entities (including lesser forms of gods such as fairies, jinns, trolls, kitsunes and more) who were brought to America through human activity. These entities are all coming from existing cultures, religions, folklores and mythologies. However they are but copies of the original entities - American avatars of a deity whose original version is still living in their homeland. Due to America being a "bad land" for gods, the Old Gods are greatly weakened by the lack of active belief or adoration, and they need to find alternative forms of worship to not fade away.
